projectDescription: SwiftUI iOS/watchOS acoustic measurement app for live microphone analysis, spectrogram and level widgets, recording, measurement metadata, export/reporting, Apple Watch companion views, and WatchKit complications.
architectureSummary: The iOS app starts in SpektoWatch2App, creates an AppServices service graph, then injects AudioEngine, recording, filtering, connectivity, FFT, and masking services into ContentView and the modular dashboard. AudioEngine owns the live AVAudioEngine tap, FFT/weighting/metrics pipeline, recording hooks, and throttled WatchConnectivity updates; FFTProcessor, FrequencyWeightingProcessor, SpectrogramProcessor, AcousticMetricsCalculator, SpectrumBandAggregator, and masking components carry the DSP work. Rendering is widget-driven: dashboard layouts persist WidgetConfiguration models, live spectrograms use HighEndSpectrogramAdapter plus Metal shaders and a ring-buffer texture, while playback/export paths use dedicated renderers. RecordingManager persists recording metadata and files, RealtimeAudioFileWriter and MeasurementDataWriter keep audio-thread disk I/O bounded, and CSV/PDF/PNG exporters read typed measurement data. The watch app has its own WatchAudioEngine for local FFT and wearable-mic mode, shared protocol/data models in Shared, and a separate WidgetKit complication extension. Tests cover DSP, watch protocol/versioning, recording, export, rendering adapters, UI flows, snapshots, and performance. The agent/ tree is an ACP planning system with milestones, tasks, reports, scripts, and progress state; M14 performance centralization is currently active.

importantDirectories.purpose: Main iOS app source, SwiftUI dashboard, audio engine, widgets, rendering, recording, export, masking, and app services.
Core DSP processors such as FFT and frequency weighting.
Shared app managers for band aggregation, bandstop filters, acoustic metrics, and generated test audio.
Adaptive masking workflow: ambient calibration, trigger acquisition, novelty detection, profile management, suggestion generation, preview playback, and masking UI/widgets.
Recording detail, playback, photo attachment, advanced analysis, bandstop UI, and supporting SwiftUI components.
Types shared by iOS, watchOS, and complications, including SpectrogramData, WatchConnectivity protocol/state, app group keys, loudness calculation, ring buffer, and widget configuration.
watchOS app entry point, WatchAudioEngine, watch dashboard/faces, watch widgets, local audio capture, and watch entitlements/assets.
WidgetKit watch complication extension with providers, entries, and complication views.
Primary unit/integration tests for audio, DSP, rendering adapters, measurement I/O, recording/export/reporting, watch connectivity, snapshots, and performance.
Additional watch-focused protocol, value mapping, FFT, and audio-engine tests.
iOS and watch UI/screenshot test targets.
Agent Context Protocol planning tree with design docs, commands, milestones, tasks, reports, schemas, scripts, and progress tracking.
Design philosophy, rendered design plates, and theme references.
HTML/CSS/JSX design handoff artifacts for the redesign and watch/complication concepts.
Stored xcresult bundles from local targeted test runs.

importantEntryPoints.purpose: iOS @main app entry; initializes AppServices, defers audio startup until first frame, and injects environment services.
Main service graph for filter, connectivity, recording, FFT, masking profile, AudioEngine, and MaskingEngine dependencies.
Root iOS view that hosts the modular dashboard over the app background.
Primary iOS dashboard shell with paged layouts, header/footer controls, widget picker, settings, and persisted widget arrangements.
Central live audio capture, FFT/weighting/metrics processing, spectrogram publication, recording coordination, and watch update throttling.
Thread-safe FFT setup, windowing, frequency bin, and dB conversion logic using Accelerate.
Bandstop filtering, octave/third-octave aggregation, binning, and temporal smoothing for spectrogram data.
Metal-backed live spectrogram MTKView with texture ring buffer, frequency mapping, colormaps, smoothing, and CADisplayLink-style rendering.
Metal vertex and fragment shaders for live and playback spectrogram rendering.
Recording metadata/file persistence, rename/delete/duplicate/photo helpers, and recording list state.
Bounded asynchronous AVAudioFile writer for audio recording without blocking the render callback.
Binary measurement frame writer for metrics, third-octave bands, and optional full FFT with bounded async disk I/O.
Typed reader for recorded measurement data used by playback, exports, and reports.
CSV and JSON measurement export from MeasurementDataReader frames.
PDF report generation with metadata, level charts, spectrogram image, third-octave charts, configuration, disclaimers, and photos.
PNG export wrapper for offline spectrogram rendering.
Adaptive masking state machine coordinating ambient calibration, trigger marking, suggestions, and preview audio-session handoff.
Shared spectrogram payload model with binary versioned encoding for WatchConnectivity.
Shared connectivity manager for spectrogram packets, recording commands, mic source selection, dashboard config, and adaptive throttling.
watchOS @main app entry; wires WatchAudioEngine and WatchConnectivityManager and handles scene backgrounding.
watchOS audio capture and local FFT/liveData pipeline with extended runtime session support and companion/wearable modes.
WidgetBundle entry for watch complications.
Xcode project and target wiring, including app, watch app, complication, tests, and Swift package dependencies.
Repository test runner script.
Top-level ACP guidance and project map for coding agents.
Current ACP milestone/task state and known gates.
ACP layout manifest and command entrypoints.

warnings: Several tests are intentionally skipped, including AudioEngine memory-management cases, an IntegrationTests race-condition stress case, Metal-dependent tests on hosts without Metal, watchOS screenshot tests without a watch simulator, and snapshot tests when baselines/package setup are unavailable.
ACP progress flags hardware-only validation gates: Apple Watch hardware acceptance, widget screenshot/behavior checks, Instruments re-render comparison, and local simulator limitations.
App Group entitlement work is documented as code-side partial/manual: entitlements files exist, but Developer Portal and Xcode target wiring remain a gate for watch complications/shared state.

The docs mention historical duplicate/legacy risk around similarly named RecordingManager and WatchConnectivityManager files/targets; target exceptions and current structure mitigate this but it remains a maintenance concern.
FULLSTACK_UEBERSICHT.md says there are no external package dependencies, but Package.resolved currently pins swift-snapshot-testing and transitive packages for tests.
Performance/centralization work is still in progress under M14; audio/render hot paths remain an active optimization area.
